req.title: Hr-Admin deletes employee
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a employee through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: Test suite validates that authenticated hr-admin users can delete employees via REST API with proper validation, audit logging, and error handling. Coverage includes: (1) positive case with valid delete and audit verification; (2) negative cases for missing fields, authentication failures, and authorization violations; (3) boundary case for edge-case employee identifiers. All tests are idempotent using unique test data and cleanup.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_employee_success
preconditions: hr-admin user is authenticated with valid JWT token
test employee with ID 'emp-test-001' exists in database
actions: Send DELETE request to /api/employees/emp-test-001 with valid authorization header
Verify response contains employee ID in body
Query audit log table for deletion event
expected: Employee record is no longer retrievable via GET /api/employees/emp-test-001
Audit record exists with action='DELETE', actor='hr-admin', resource_id='emp-test-001'
trace: R-WEB-003: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_hr_admin_deletes_employee_success():
    import requests
    token = <get_valid_hr_admin_token>
    emp_id = 'emp-test-001'
    <create_test_employee(emp_id)>
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    resp = requests.delete(f'{BASE_URL}/api/employees/{emp_id}', headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    assert resp.json()['employee_id'] == emp_id
    get_resp = requests.get(f'{BASE_URL}/api/employees/{emp_id}', headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 404
    audit = <query_audit_log(action='DELETE', resource_id=emp_id)>
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit['actor'] == 'hr-admin'

tests.name: test_delete_employee_missing_required_field_id
actions: Send DELETE request to /api/employees with empty employee_id path parameter
expected: Response contains error message indicating missing or invalid employee_id
tests.executable:
def test_delete_employee_missing_required_field_id():
    import requests
    token = <get_valid_hr_admin_token>
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    resp = requests.delete(f'{BASE_URL}/api/employees/', headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert 'employee_id' in resp.json()['error'].lower() or 'required' in resp.json()['error'].lower()
    audit = <query_audit_log(action='DELETE')>
    assert len(audit) == 0

tests.name: test_delete_employee_unauthenticated
actions: Send DELETE request to /api/employees/{employee_id} without authorization header
tests.executable:
def test_delete_employee_unauthenticated():
    import requests
    emp_id = 'emp-test-002'
    <create_test_employee(emp_id)>
    resp = requests.delete(f'{BASE_URL}/api/employees/{emp_id}')
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    get_resp = requests.get(f'{BASE_URL}/api/employees/{emp_id}')
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    <cleanup_test_employee(emp_id)>

tests.name: test_delete_employee_insufficient_role
actions: Send DELETE request to /api/employees/{employee_id} with non-admin user token
trace: R-WEB-003: error pathway for forbidden role
tests.executable:
def test_delete_employee_insufficient_role():
    import requests
    token = <get_valid_employee_token>
    emp_id = 'emp-test-003'
    <create_test_employee(emp_id)>
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    resp = requests.delete(f'{BASE_URL}/api/employees/{emp_id}', headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    get_resp = requests.get(f'{BASE_URL}/api/employees/{emp_id}')
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    <cleanup_test_employee(emp_id)>

tests.name: test_delete_nonexistent_employee
expected: Response contains error indicating employee not found
No audit record created for non-existent resource
trace: R-WEB-003: boundary case for non-existent resource
tests.executable:
def test_delete_nonexistent_employee():
    import requests
    token = <get_valid_hr_admin_token>
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    emp_id = 'emp-nonexistent-9999'
    resp = requests.delete(f'{BASE_URL}/api/employees/{emp_id}', headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 404
    assert 'not found' in resp.json()['error'].lower()

tests.name: test_delete_employee_invalid_id_format
actions: Send DELETE request with malformed employee_id (special characters, SQL injection attempt)
expected: Response contains validation error for invalid ID format
No SQL injection or command execution occurs
trace: R-WEB-003: boundary case for invalid format
